condensed_reasoning:
## Research

I set out to forecast whether **OpenAI**, **Anthropic**, or **Mistral AI** would have a funding round dated **July 2026** on TracXn. I fired two parallel research queries: one on recent/pending 2026 funding activity for all three, and one on **how TracXn actually lists rounds** (tranches, debt, secondaries) and the historical monthly base rate. I supplemented with direct web searches per company and attempted an AskNews recency check (which failed — out of credits) and a TracXn WebFetch (which returned only JS, no data).

## Key Evidence

- **OpenAI**: Closed its record **$122B Series F** at an **$852B** valuation (Feb–Mar 2026), then went **IPO-track** — confidentially filed an S-1 (~May 22, 2026), targeting a Q4 2026 debut. No new private round pending.
- **Anthropic**: Closed **$65B Series H** at a **$965B** valuation, announced late May 2026 and described as **likely its last private raise before IPO**. No new round pending.
- **Mistral AI**: The **only live candidate** — a rumored **~€3B raise at ~€20B valuation**, first reported June 12, 2026, but still in **"early discussions"** with no lead investor or close as of July 4, 2026. Its most recent *completed* round was $830M debt financing (March 30, 2026).
- **TracXn listing behavior**: Logs debt, grants, "Others," undisclosed rounds, and **small tranches** as separate dated rows (OpenAI's Series F appears 3× — Feb $122B, Mar $40M, Apr $75M). Historical monthly hit rates: **~11% of OpenAI's months, ~30% of Anthropic's**, sharply accelerating in early 2026 (~6 combined entries across 8 company-months in Jan–Apr).
- **Critical table detail**: The actual TracXn tables show the **most recent dated entry for BOTH OpenAI and Anthropic is April 2026** — despite Series H being *announced* late May, TracXn dated it April. So **May and June 2026 have zero new dated entries** for either.

## Conclusion

My first pass anchored on the boom-era combined base rate (~45%) and landed at a **reflection estimate of 0.47**. The premortem review flagged that this over-weighted the unconditional base rate and under-weighted the **observed two-month (May/June) zero-entry drought** immediately after the mega-round cluster — which directly undercuts the "tranche follow-ons bleed into adjacent months" argument, since the follow-ons had already stopped. I accepted this and rebuilt forward from current state per-company: **OpenAI ~7%, Anthropic ~7%, Mistral ~17%** → 1 − (0.93 × 0.93 × 0.83) ≈ 0.29, plus a small uplift for TracXn dating lags and backfill noise → I concluded **0.34 (34%)**. Mistral's early-stage €3B round is the main path to YES but is unlikely to close and be dated within ~6 weeks.

capability_gaps: A working recency-news source (AskNews was out of credits) would have let the agent confirm or rule out any Mistral close in the July 5–31 window — the single largest residual blind spot.
A reliable structured TracXn scrape (the WebFetch returned only JS) would have removed reliance on search snippets for the exact dated rows.
subtle_bugs: The Bloomberg source URLs consistently returned null snippets in web_search results, so the agent relied on secondary aggregators (TechCrunch, MLQ, StartupHub) to confirm the Mistral round details — not misleading here, but a pattern worth noting.

classification.approach: Per-company build-forward probability from current funding state plus TracXn base-rate context, combined as independent complements.
classification.key_uncertainty: Whether Mistral's early-stage €3B round closes and gets a July-2026 TracXn date within a ~6-week window, plus TracXn's idiosyncratic month-dating conventions.

risk_flags.evidence: The WebFetch to the live TracXn Mistral page returned only JS/CSS (no funding data), and AskNews was out of credits, so no source captures any Mistral close between July 5–31, 2026. The forecast relies on data through July 4, 2026 with a residual blind spot for the back ~3.5 weeks of the resolution month.

The initial reflection anchored at ~45% by summing per-company unconditional boom-era monthly hit rates (Anthropic 30% + OpenAI 11% + Mistral ~15%) without conditioning on the observed post-round May/June drought. This was caught by the premortem and corrected downward to 0.34, but the initial anchor was a genuine base-rate misuse.

strengths: Rebuilt the estimate from current state after the premortem flagged the May/June zero-entry drought, moving from 0.47 to 0.34 rather than parking near the boom-era anchor — a genuine, evidence-driven revision.

Extracted and used the actual TracXn funding tables (with exact dated rows: Anthropic Apr 21 2026, OpenAI Apr 22 2026) rather than relying on summary snippets, which surfaced the decisive 'last entry = April' observation.

Correctly identified that the question hinges on TracXn's idiosyncratic month-dating (e.g., Series H announced late May but dated 'Apr 2026'), and explicitly folded that dating-lag uncertainty into the final number.
Cleanly separated the three companies: two IPO-track with closed final rounds (~7% each) vs. Mistral as the sole live catalyst (~17%), then combined as independent complements.
weaknesses: The premortem tool call first failed with a Pydantic validation error (missing what_would_change_my_mind and confidence_in_estimate fields because they were malformed into the counterargument string), requiring a retry — wasted a round-trip.

The Mistral ~17% leg is asserted rather than modeled; no explicit reasoning class of 'time from early-discussions-report to dated funding entry' (e.g., how long Mistral's own Series C or comparable mega-rounds took) was constructed to calibrate the ~6-week close probability.

The initial reflection (0.47) required an external premortem prompt to catch the drought signal that was already sitting in the agent's own extracted TracXn table — the agent had the data but did not weight it correctly on the first pass.

issues: AskNews MCP returned 403 (out of credits) on both calls, forcing a pivot to web_search and leaving the July 5-31 recency window unconfirmed.

First mcp__premortem__premortem call failed with a Pydantic validation error because required fields (what_would_change_my_mind, confidence_in_estimate) were embedded as text inside the counterargument string rather than passed as separate arguments — required a retry.
A PreToolUse hook denied a Bash 'ls/find' command in the premortem agent; the agent recovered via Grep. Full-file Read exceeded the token limit, also handled via fallback.
